description_long: This sculpture depicts a centaur, a mythological creature with the upper body of a human and the lower body of a horse, standing on a gently curved rectangular base. The human torso is upright, with one arm bent and the other extended outward, suggesting a gesture or greeting. The horse body is robust, with well-defined musculature and a textured tail, while the human figure is smooth and stylized. The surface of the sculpture has a weathered, patinated bronze appearance, with some areas showing lighter patches, possibly due to wear or artistic intent. The base is made of a solid, light-colored stone or concrete, providing stability and contrast to the darker figure. The overall impression is one of strength and grace, blending human and equine forms in a dynamic pose.
description: A patinated bronze centaur sculpture stands on a curved stone base, blending human and horse forms with a dynamic, gestural pose.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the centaur's human torso, extended arm, and the full curve of the base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the centaur's back, tail, and the curvature of the base.
description_view_2: Left side view displaying the centaur's profile, musculature, and a large lighter patch on the horse body.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the centaur's face, chest, and the forward-reaching arm.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is mounted on a stone base, suggesting an outdoor or public installation, possibly in a park or plaza.
